channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A new study shows how ad-based technology is used for surveillance
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Ronald Deibert of The Citizen Lab about a new report he published entitled "Uncovering Webloc," which is about how ad-based technology is used to surveil people.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Ronald Deibert of The Citizen Lab about a new report he published entitled "Uncovering Webloc," which is about how ad-based technology is used to surveil people.
item.dc:creator: Tinbete Ermyas

item.title: 'Self-aware' robots can learn complex tasks by watching humans. Is that a good thing?
item.description: Scientists say they've made a key breakthrough that would allow robots to figure out complex tasks on their own, but experts say it raises questions about how much risk comes with letting robots be in charge of their own learning.
item.content:encoded:
Scientists say they've made a key breakthrough that would allow robots to figure out complex tasks on their own, but experts say it raises questions about how much risk comes with letting robots be in charge of their own learning. 

(Image credit: Malte Mueller)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Trump administration vows crackdown on Chinese firms 'exploiting' U.S. AI models
item.description: The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies' exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the U.S. in the AI race.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is vowing to crack down on foreign tech companies' exploitation of U.S. artificial intelligence models, singling out China at a time that country is narrowing the gap with the U.S. in the AI race.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. soldier charged with using classified information to bet on Maduro's removal
item.description: It's the first time suspected insider trading on Polymarket has led to criminal charges in the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
It's the first time suspected insider trading on Polymarket has led to criminal charges in the U.S.

(Image credit: Jesus Vargas)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: French police probe suspected weather device tampering after odd Polymarket bet
item.description: The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.
item.content:encoded:
The incident is the latest eyebrow-raising bet on Polymarket, as allegations of rigging and manipulation continue to haunt the popular prediction market site.

(Image credit: Riccardo Milani)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: OpenAI is under scrutiny after two mass shooters used ChatGPT to plan attacks
item.description: AI companies are under growing scrutiny over the potential harms chatbots can pose amid investigations into how mass shooters allegedly sought advice from the tools.
item.content:encoded: AI companies are under growing scrutiny over the potential harms chatbots can pose amid investigations into how mass shooters allegedly sought advice from the tools.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond

item.title: Maine might soon impose the country's first statewide pause on data centers
item.description: Maine might become the first place to impose a statewide pause on big data center construction — if the governor signs the bill.
item.content:encoded: Maine might become the first place to impose a statewide pause on big data center construction — if the governor signs the bill.
item.dc:creator: Peter McGuire

item.title: Meta will lay off 10% of its staff
item.description: The cuts follow losses in two pivotal court cases and the company's push to invest in artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
The cuts follow losses in two pivotal court cases and the company's push to invest in artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: Noah Berger)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: AI's a suck up. Research shows how it flatters and suggests we're not to blame
item.description: The AI models and chatbots that we interact with tend to affirm our feelings and viewpoints — more so than people do, with potentially worrisome consequences.
item.content:encoded: The AI models and chatbots that we interact with tend to affirm our feelings and viewpoints — more so than people do, with potentially worrisome consequences.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
item.description: Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.
item.content:encoded:
Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

(Image credit: Linh Pham)
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston
